text.title: MLP Planning Core Rules
text.summary: Four-layer Minimum Lovable Product audit (Functional -> Reliable -> Usable -> Delightful) for evolving an MVP that users call 'fine' into one they love and recommend.

rule.statement: MLP plan MUST audit all four layers (Functional, Reliable, Usable, Delightful) and assign a score 0-5 per layer with cited evidence (metric or quote).

rationale.source: Aarron Walter — Designing for Emotion; Henrik Kniberg MLP essay

rule.rationale: Single-layer focus on broken Functional/Reliable layers wastes budget; full audit prevents top-of-pyramid investment on a shaky base.

rule.statement: Delight investments are forbidden when Functional or Reliable score < 3. Fix the base first.

rationale.source: Maslow-style needs hierarchy applied to product

rule.rationale: Delight on a buggy product amplifies frustration; users notice polish on broken flows more, not less.

rule.statement: Delight items MUST cite verbatim user quotes or moment-of-truth observations, not designer opinion.
rule.rationale: Designer-opinion delight ships features the team loves and users don't notice.

rule.statement: MLP success metric is the Day-30 retention curve, not feature throughput. The plan MUST define the curve target before sprint kickoff.

rationale.source: Andrew Chen — Mobile App Retention research

rule.rationale: Without a curve target, MLP work shows up as activity, not impact.

rule.statement: Each delight sprint MUST be <=2 weeks with a single named theme (onboarding, empty-state, completion). Open-ended delight work decays into polish-by-vibe.
rule.rationale: Open-ended delight backlogs accumulate but ship slowly; bounded sprints force scope and ship-rate.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — pre-MVP without Functional shipped; commodity competing on price; B2B with buyer != user; Day-30 retention already >40%), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
